disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden onset of severe, localized pain in one testicle about two hours ago
trigger_question_examples: When did the pain start and how quickly did it come on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on really suddenly about two hours ago in my left testicle — one minute it was the usual dull ache, the next it exploded into the worst pain I've ever had.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A hard, heavy lump in the testicle present for about three weeks before tonight
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any lump or change down there before tonight?
How long has the lump been there?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's been a hard, heavy lump on that testicle for about three weeks. It wasn't sore before — just felt firm and heavy, like a dragging weight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Constant sensation of heaviness and a dull ache in the scrotum before the acute pain
trigger_question_examples: What did it feel like before tonight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's felt heavy and achy in the scrotum the whole time — a constant dull dragging feeling on that side.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Mild tenderness and swelling of both breasts developing over the last month
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any changes anywhere else in your body lately?
Any tenderness or swelling in your chest or breasts?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now you mention it — my chest, both sides, has felt a bit tender and slightly swollen over the last month. I thought it was nothing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No burning on urination, no urethral discharge, and no fevers or chills
trigger_question_examples: Any burning or stinging when you pass urine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — no burning when I wee, no discharge, and I haven't had any fevers or chills.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No recent injury, trauma, or strenuous activity before the pain started
trigger_question_examples: Did you hurt or knock yourself before this started?
Were you doing anything strenuous when the pain came on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I didn't knock it or lift anything or do any sport — it just came on by itself while I was resting.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient is sweaty and pale, lying still with hips bent to ease the pain
trigger_question_examples: How are you coping right now — can you describe how you look and feel?
What position are you in, and are you sweaty or pale?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm sweating and I feel pale and clammy from the pain. I'm lying on my side with my hips bent up because that's the only way I can stand it.
